title: Satanocrater coccineus
intro: Satanocrater coccineus, also known as red devil's claw, is a flowering plant species in the family Campanulaceae. Traditionally, Satanocrater coccineus has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including pain and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Satanocrater coccineus include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Satanocrater coccineus is often combined with other plants to create teas, tinctures, and capsules that aim to alleviate pain and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Satanocrater coccineus is classified as a species within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It belongs to the genus Satanocrater, which is a small group of flowering plants. The species is native to tropical Africa, specifically in the countries of Cameroon and Equatorial Guinea. Satanocrater coccineus is a terrestrial orchid, which means it grows on the ground rather than on other plants. Further classification within the kingdom of plants is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Monocots, order Asparagales, family Orchidaceae, genus Satanocrater, species Satanocrater coccineus.
